insight: In Vivid Drone sets with high economy, the 1EE 1DD opening is superior to DD DDEE for P2 because it generates 1 extra gold on turn 3 (12 gold vs 11), enabling access to stronger turn 3 plays like DDDD or 1DDC that the DD DDEE line cannot afford, despite DD DDEE being less committal on turn 1.
author: 307th, mtanzer
context: Discussion of P2 opening lines in high-econ sets with Vivid Drone present.

insight: Vivid Drone has an energy cost of 2, making it more efficient than regular Drone; an Engineer purchased specifically to generate energy for Vivids effectively generates 1 gold per turn in net econ advantage after ~2 turns of Vivid production.
author: 307th
context: Discussion of when to buy Engineers to support Vivid Drone economy.

insight: Sacrificing an Engineer to allow favorable positioning (e.g., getting a Blastforge online while opponent builds Gauss Cannon) is often correct play, as Engineers are the cheapest unit to lose and defending against breach on the next turn is more important than maintaining economy.
author: 307th, sunfallte
context: Analysis of Lifecoach vs Swim game where sacrificing an Engineer was part of optimal strategy.

insight: Units that finish constructing during a player's turn are constructed before 'start of turn' effects are calculated; Amporilla gains attack from all Tarsiers built that turn, not just attacking ones.
author: http404error
context: Clarification of event ordering: Build phase occurs before start-of-turn effects.

insight: Cryo Ray's value depends heavily on situation; as a freeze mechanic, it is generally weaker than Gauss Cannon because Gauss Cannon permanently removes blockers while Cryo Ray only temporarily freezes them, making Gauss Cannon superior for forcing breaches in most concrete scenarios.
author: mrguy888
context: Extended debate on Cryo Ray vs Gauss Cannon efficiency and tactical application.

insight: In sets with Wild Drone available, buying 2-3 Wild Drones is typically efficient, but more than that becomes vulnerable to opponent pressure; strong players sometimes deliberately avoid overbuying Wild Drones to make their opponent's early attack less effective.
author: strshplyr5046, mqp
context: Discussion of Wild Drone purchasing strategy and economy efficiency.

insight: In Redeemer sets where P1 gets natural access to Redeemer, the matchup is not as heavily P2-favored as commonly believed; P1 can play competitively with a DDB opening, and alternative P1 lines like DD DDE can work depending on other unit support in the set.
author: mtanzer, mqp
context: Pushback against perception that Redeemer is overwhelmingly P2-favored.

insight: Splitter is a useful unit in Redeemer mirrors because 1 damage is a better damage number to hit than 3; it blocks effectively on incoming Redeemer charge turns and can attack on other turns, enabling flexible openers like DDB into DCS into WCD.
author: mqp
context: Analysis of Splitter's role in Redeemer set openers.

insight: In high-economy sets, P2 often makes suboptimal opening choices like DEE 1DD or DD DDEE instead of the correct 1EE 1DD, and this is because most players default to less-committal early plays and don't calculate the turn 3 advantage; this pattern contributes to skewed win rates in Vivid Drone sets.
author: 307th
context: Explanation for why Vivid Drone had high P1 win rate despite balanced design.

insight: In the Amporilla set analysis, both players getting Amporilla in a sharp tempo race is likely optimal play with best play, with the game revolving around stacking Tarsiers while defending; Gaussite Symbiote can disrupt opponent Amporilla econ but Deadeyes are too slow to prevent it.
author: mqp
context: Strategic assessment of complex set with Amporilla and multiple pressure options.
